name: Half-timber wall
description: Half-timber (Fachwerk) wall section emulating the exposed-frame construction of medieval Tudor and German cottages. A dark wooden skeleton of vertical studs and horizontal rails is laid against the wall plane, dividing it into rectangular panels; the interstitial cells are then back-filled with a cream stucco-like infill (white terracotta, wattle-and-daub equivalent). The result is the iconic dark-on-cream lattice visible across the facade. Designed to be tiled along a perimeter in 1-block-thick sections of variable height, so the architecture_planner can apply it to any straight wall segment without altering the room footprint behind it.
